content: Butler, though he himself belonged to a great Anglo-Irish dynasty, was presumably considered to be a loyal servant of the Crown: unlike many of their peers, they had supported the House of Lancaster , from whom the Tudor dynasty derived its claim to the Throne
summary: Butler, though he himself belonged to a great Anglo-Irish dynasty, was presumably considered to be a loyal servant of the Crown: unlike many of their peers, they had supported the House of Lancaster , from whom the Tudor dynasty derived its claim to the Throne
